content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 25, 2001

Tina M. Wallis
Hatch and Parent
Post Office Drawer 720
Santa Barbara, California 93102-0720

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-01-094

Dear Ms. Wallis:

We have received your April 24, 2001 request for advice on behalf of Eric
Schafer regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ As mentioned in our telephone conversation, the Commission may decline to
issue advice where the question presented is hypothetical. (Regulation 18329(b)(8),
enclosed.) The Commission also may decline to render advice if it appears that the
material facts provided in the request may be inaccurate, incomplete, or in dispute.
(Ibid.)

We have determined that your request either presents hypothetical questions or
does not provide the material facts needed by us to render advice to you. Consequently,
the Commission declines to issue advice on this matter.

Please do not hesitate to write in again once you have identified a specific
governmental decision before Mr. Schafer's agency.

_______________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-01-094
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Natalie Bocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

NB:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\01-094
Enclosure

sections.conclusion: We have determined that your request either presents hypothetical questions or does not provide the material facts needed by us to render advice to you. Consequently, the Commission declines to issue advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: The request sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act for Eric Schafer, but the FPPC determined the questions were hypothetical or lacked sufficient material facts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to issue advice because the request presents hypothetical questions or lacks sufficient material facts needed for analysis. The requester is invited to resubmit once a specific governmental decision is identified.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The question and conclusion information were extracted from the body text. Document states it may be hypothetical or factually incomplete.

QUESTION: The request sought advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act for Eric Schafer, but the FPPC determined the questions were hypothetical or lacked sufficient material facts.

CONCLUSION: We have determined that your request either presents hypothetical questions or does not provide the material facts needed by us to render advice to you. Consequently, the Commission declines to issue advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue an advice letter on a conflict-of-interest matter for Eric Schafer because the request presented hypothetical questions and lacked necessary material facts. The requestor was advised to resubmit once a specific governmental decision was identified.
